---
title: "Algebra Repair"
description: "A repair-first module for expressions, equations, functions, exponents, trigonometric language, and symbol fluency before heavier theory."
page-type: module-overview
status: published
update-status: current
level: foundation
topic: algebra-repair
prerequisites:
  - /start-here/notation-and-symbols.qmd
learning-goals:
  - "Repair the algebra habits that most often block later math modules."
  - "Know the intended first-pass direction of the algebra-repair module."
  - "Reconnect symbolic fluency to graphs, growth, and later technical reading."
applications:
  - calculus
  - probability
  - optimization
keywords:
  - algebra
  - equations
  - functions
  - logarithms
  - symbolic manipulation
maintainer: mtuann
last-reviewed: 2026-04-25
source-check-date: 2026-04-25
review-cadence: annual
date-modified: last-modified
draft: false
stable-sources:
  - "OpenStax"
  - "Khan Academy"
  - "Paul's Online Math Notes"
current-sources:
  - "OpenStax College Algebra 2e"
  - "OpenStax Precalculus 2e"
  - "Khan Academy Algebra basics"
  - "Khan Academy Algebra 1"
  - "Paul's Notes Algebra"
  - "Paul's Notes Function intro"
  - "Paul's Notes Exponential/Log Functions"
  - "Paul's Notes Growth/Decay"
  - "Khan Academy Trigonometry"
  - "Khan Academy Precalculus"
  - "Paul's Notes Trig Functions"
  - "Paul's Notes Complex Numbers"
  - "CMU 15-151 course page"
---

## Why This Module Matters

This module exists for one very practical reason:

many later math problems are not blocked by the advanced idea yet. They are blocked by algebra friction.

That friction shows up as:

- dropping minus signs
- treating equivalent transformations carelessly
- losing track of what a function takes in and returns
- misreading exponential or logarithmic growth
- getting stuck in symbolic manipulation before the real math even starts

Without this repair layer, later modules like calculus, probability, optimization, information theory, and learning theory often feel harder than they really are.

::: {.meta-strip}
::: {.meta-box}
**Prerequisites**
[Notation and symbols](../../../start-here/notation-and-symbols.qmd), patience, and willingness to slow down
:::
::: {.meta-box}
**Unlocks**
Proofs, calculus, probability, linear algebra, optimization
:::
::: {.meta-box}
**Research Use**
Reading formulas cleanly, checking derivations, simplifying expressions, and avoiding symbol-level mistakes
:::
:::

## First Pass Through This Module

The intended first-pass spine is:

1. [Expressions and Equations](concepts/expressions-and-equations.qmd)
2. [Functions and Graph Reading](concepts/functions-and-graph-reading.qmd)
3. [Exponents, Logarithms, and Growth](concepts/exponents-logarithms-and-growth.qmd)
4. [Trig and Complex Numbers](concepts/trig-and-complex-numbers.qmd)
5. [Symbol Manipulation Lab](concepts/symbol-manipulation-lab.qmd)

This now gives the module a complete first-pass spine for the algebra blockages that most often slow down later theory.

## How To Use This Module

The best opening path is:

1. start with [Expressions and Equations](concepts/expressions-and-equations.qmd)
2. continue to [Functions and Graph Reading](concepts/functions-and-graph-reading.qmd)
3. continue to [Exponents, Logarithms, and Growth](concepts/exponents-logarithms-and-growth.qmd)
4. continue to [Trig and Complex Numbers](concepts/trig-and-complex-numbers.qmd)
5. finish with [Symbol Manipulation Lab](concepts/symbol-manipulation-lab.qmd)
6. keep [Notation and symbols](../../../start-here/notation-and-symbols.qmd) nearby if syntax itself is still slowing you down
7. return to [Proofs](../proofs/index.qmd) or [Single-Variable Calculus](../single-variable-calculus/index.qmd) once symbolic steps stop feeling fragile

This module is deliberately repair-oriented. It is not trying to be an algebra textbook from scratch. It is trying to remove the exact kinds of friction that keep later pages from feeling readable.

## Core Concepts

- [Expressions and Equations](concepts/expressions-and-equations.qmd): the opening repair page for variable meaning, equivalent transformations, solving equations, and common algebra mistakes.
- [Functions and Graph Reading](concepts/functions-and-graph-reading.qmd): the second repair page for input-output thinking, domain/range, composition, and graph interpretation.
- [Exponents, Logarithms, and Growth](concepts/exponents-logarithms-and-growth.qmd): the third repair page for exponent rules, logarithms, inverse thinking, and growth comparison.
- [Trig and Complex Numbers](concepts/trig-and-complex-numbers.qmd): the fourth repair page for radian language, sine/cosine interpretation, and complex-number basics.
- [Symbol Manipulation Lab](concepts/symbol-manipulation-lab.qmd): the closing repair page for multi-step drills, factor-before-cancel habits, and symbolic hygiene.

## After This First Pass

The strongest adjacent next moves are:

- [Proofs](../proofs/index.qmd)
- [Single-Variable Calculus](../single-variable-calculus/index.qmd)
- [Probability](../probability/index.qmd)
- [Linear Algebra](../linear-algebra/index.qmd)

## Applications

### Calculus Readiness

Derivatives and integrals become much easier once symbolic transformations stop consuming all the attention.

### Probability And Statistics

Expectation formulas, densities, likelihoods, and Bayes updates all rely on calm algebra more than they first appear to.

### Optimization And Modeling

Much of optimization starts as algebra on objective functions, constraints, and parameterizations.

## Go Deeper By Topic

### Equation Solving

Start with [Expressions and Equations](concepts/expressions-and-equations.qmd).

If that page still feels shaky, slow down and work one problem where you:

- simplify an expression
- solve a linear equation
- solve a rational equation
- explain why each transformation preserved the solution set

That explanation step matters as much as the final answer.

### Function Language

Continue to [Functions and Graph Reading](concepts/functions-and-graph-reading.qmd).

If that page still feels shaky, work one example where you:

- identify the domain
- describe the range
- compute one composition
- explain what the graph means in words instead of only drawing it

### Exponents And Logarithms

Continue to [Exponents, Logarithms, and Growth](concepts/exponents-logarithms-and-growth.qmd).

If that page still feels shaky, work one example where you:

- rewrite numbers in a common base
- solve one simple exponential equation
- explain a logarithm as an inverse statement
- compare `\log n`, `n`, and `2^n` in words

### Trig And Complex Numbers

Continue to [Trig and Complex Numbers](concepts/trig-and-complex-numbers.qmd).

If that page still feels shaky, work one example where you:

- read sine and cosine from the unit circle
- switch between degrees and radians
- plot a complex number as a point
- compute its modulus and explain the geometry

### Symbolic Hygiene

Finish with [Symbol Manipulation Lab](concepts/symbol-manipulation-lab.qmd).

If that page still feels shaky, work one example where you:

- mark restrictions
- factor before cancelling
- simplify one line at a time
- explain why the final form is equivalent to the original one

## Optional Deeper Reading After First Pass

- [OpenStax College Algebra 2e](https://openstax.org/details/books/college-algebra-2e) - broad open official text for equations, functions, and symbolic fluency. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [OpenStax Precalculus 2e](https://openstax.org/details/books/precalculus-2e) - strong open official reference for algebra-to-precalculus repair. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [Khan Academy Algebra basics](https://www.khanacademy.org/math/algebra-basics) - useful official practice-oriented reinforcement when fluency is weaker than conceptual understanding. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [Paul's Notes Function intro](https://tutorial.math.lamar.edu/Classes/Alg/Functions.aspx) - stable worked-example reinforcement for function notation and graph interpretation. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [Paul's Notes Exponential/Log Functions](https://tutorial.math.lamar.edu/Classes/Alg/ExpLogFcns.aspx) - stable worked-example reinforcement for exponentials, logarithms, and inverse thinking. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [Khan Academy Trigonometry](https://www.khanacademy.org/math/trigonometry) - official practice-oriented reinforcement for radians, unit-circle thinking, and trig functions. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [Paul's Notes Algebra](https://tutorial.math.lamar.edu/Classes/Alg/Alg.aspx) - stable worked-example reinforcement for multi-step symbolic manipulation. Checked `2026-04-25`.

## Sources and Further Reading

- [OpenStax College Algebra 2e](https://openstax.org/details/books/college-algebra-2e) - `First pass` - open official algebra text for equations, functions, and symbolic structure. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [OpenStax Precalculus 2e](https://openstax.org/details/books/precalculus-2e) - `Second pass` - open official reference for algebra repair that continues naturally into trigonometry and growth. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [Khan Academy Algebra basics](https://www.khanacademy.org/math/algebra-basics) - `First pass` - official skill-based practice layer for repairing fragile algebra habits. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [Khan Academy Algebra 1](https://www.khanacademy.org/math/algebra) - `Second pass` - broader official practice hub for equation solving and graph reading. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [Paul's Notes Algebra](https://tutorial.math.lamar.edu/Classes/Alg/Alg.aspx) - `Second pass` - stable reference for worked examples and symbolic manipulation patterns. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [Paul's Notes Function intro](https://tutorial.math.lamar.edu/Classes/Alg/Functions.aspx) - `First pass` - stable worked-example page for function notation, domain/range, and graph interpretation. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [Paul's Notes Exponential/Log Functions](https://tutorial.math.lamar.edu/Classes/Alg/ExpLogFcns.aspx) - `First pass` - stable worked-example page for exponents, logarithms, and inverse interpretation. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [Khan Academy Trigonometry](https://www.khanacademy.org/math/trigonometry) - `First pass` - official skill-based reinforcement for trig-function and unit-circle fluency. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [CMU 15-151 course page](https://www.cs.cmu.edu/~15151/) - `Paper bridge` - theory-oriented course page reminding why algebra fluency matters before proof-heavy discrete math. Checked `2026-04-25`.

Sources checked online on `2026-04-25`:

- OpenStax College Algebra 2e
- OpenStax Precalculus 2e
- Khan Academy Algebra basics
- Khan Academy Algebra 1
- Paul's Notes Algebra
- Paul's Notes Function intro
- Paul's Notes Exponential/Log Functions
- Khan Academy Trigonometry
- CMU 15-151 course page
